companies_typically_follow_a_hierarchica: Companies typically follow a hierarchical structure with a board, executives, and functional departments, though variations exist like functional, divisional, or matrix models. This setup ensures clear roles, accountability, and efficiency in achieving business goals. Top Leadership The board of directors and shareholders provide oversight and funding, setting strategic direction. CEOs lead overall operations and performance, while CFOs manage finances and risks—both critical for long-term success and investor confidence. COOs handle day-to-day execution, ensuring operational efficiency
